title: tpm — project_tracking_weekly_stakeholder_meeting_email — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=project_tracking_weekly_stakeholder_meeting_email. Refine before promoting.

project_name.description: Extract the program/project name from the Confluence page title and/or the Project Overview/Scope section; if multiple names appear, choose the one that best describes the initiative in plain language (expand acronyms on first use).

meeting_context.description: Write a 1–2 sentence context line for the weekly stakeholder meeting pre-read in a formal voice, referencing the project name and that this email is the pre-read for the upcoming weekly stakeholder sync; do not include or invent a meeting date/time.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single Red/Amber/Green status by aggregating the WBS task statuses, due dates, and the latest dated Task Status Updates; always include a 1-sentence justification grounded in what is on-track vs slipping and any open blockers/risks.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health by summarising milestone/due-date adherence from the Key Milestone section plus WBS due dates and dated status updates; state whether the plan is on track, at risk, or slipping and cite the most relevant date(s) driving that assessment.

blocking_issues.description: Derive blockers by aggregating items explicitly labelled as issues/blockers and any WBS rows or status updates indicating 'blocked', 'failed', 'waiting on', or requiring fixes; for each blocker include: description, owner (team/person), target_resolution (date if stated), and critical_path (true if it gates a milestone or multiple downstream tasks). Return an empty array if none are evidenced.

dependencies.description: Derive dependencies by aggregating the WBS dependency column/rows and any narrative dependency statements in updates (e.g., 'depends on tool change'); for each dependency include: depends_on (what), blocked_by (if stated), owner, and status (on-track/at-risk/blocked) based on the latest update text.

risks.description: Derive risks by aggregating any risk statements and implied risks from status updates (e.g., testing not complete, rollout date TBD); express each as 'X is at risk because Y; mitigation is Z.' Include owner if stated; do not fabricate mitigations—use 'mitigation TBD' if none is provided.

next_steps.description: Derive next steps by aggregating explicit action items and forward-looking statements in Task Status Updates and WBS notes; each item must include: action, owner, and due (a date if stated, otherwise 'this week'/'this sprint' as evidenced). Keep to the top 5–10 most material items for exec readability.

exec_asks.description: Derive exec asks by aggregating any requests for decisions, approvals, resourcing, escalations, or cross-team unblocks found in status updates, dependencies, and blockers; rewrite them as clear asks with: ask, requested_of (role/team if stated, otherwise 'Stakeholders'), and needed_by (date if stated, otherwise 'before next sync'). Return an empty array if none are evidenced.

stakeholder_pocs.description: Extract stakeholder points of contact from the Stakeholders/Contacts table; include name, email (if present), and role/team. Use this to populate the 'To:' suggestion list for the email draft (do not guess missing emails).

reference_links.description: Extract the most relevant pre-read links from the tracking page: design/architecture docs, repository, Jira links (epics/bugs), test plan link, and Slack channel; include label + URL (or Confluence link text) and keep to the top 8 links.

team_status_prompt_template.description: Generate an exec/POC-ready reply template that prompts each team to provide: status, blockers, risks, dependencies, actions, owners, and ETAs; format as a compact table teams can reply inline with (Team | Status | Blockers | Risks | Deps | Actions/Owners/ETAs).

email_subject.description: Generate the email subject exactly as: Weekly status for project : <Title of the confluence page>. If multiple Confluence URLs/pages are provided, make a best-judgment call on the most appropriate overall project name/title and use that in the subject.

email_body.description: Compose the full pre-read agenda email in a formal, exec-safe voice: include meeting_context, RAG + justification, schedule_health, top blockers, top risks, key dependencies, next steps, exec asks, and reference_links; end with the team_status_prompt_template and a clear request to reply with updates before the meeting. Keep scannable with headings and bullets.

orm_ticket.description: Extract the ORM ticket/link if explicitly present (e.g., an ORM ID or URL). If the page only contains a placeholder (e.g., '<insert ORM ticket>') or no ORM reference, return an empty string.
